title: Japanese Custard Pudding
description: A rich Japanese dessert made with a custard base and a caramelized sugar crust. This recipe is easy to make and perfect for special occasions.

instructions.step: In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, cornstarch, and 1/2 cup of milk. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and the remaining 1/2 cup of milk. Temper the egg yolks by slowly pouring the hot sugar mixture into the egg yolks, whisking constantly.
Return the saucepan to the stovetop and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the vanilla extract and butter until melted.
Strain the custard mixture into 4-6 ramekins or small baking dishes.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the custard is set and the tops are golden brown.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath and let cool to room temperature. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Serve the Japanese custard pudding chilled, garnished with caramelized sugar if desired.
